behavior_summary: U8P_EWBIGWEB::gotHit is a process invoked when the entity/item is hit; its observable effect is to immediately destroy the instance.
No conditional checks, dialogue, flag reads/writes, or branching are present in the decompiled nodes — destruction occurs unconditionally when the process runs.
Parameters (uword, word) are present but unused in the visible code; their semantic role must be inferred from naming and engine conventions rather than from this script.
notable_control_flow: Process-oriented entry: U8P_EWBIGWEB::gotHit runs as a process (processType 0x020C) when the object receives a hit/damage event.
Single linear action: the function calls Item::destroy(this) and then terminates; there are no branches or guards inside U8P_EWBIGWEB::gotHit.
No global flag manipulation or menu/dialogue interaction is performed by this script.
open_questions: What semantic information is carried in the parameters (uword, word) passed to U8P_EWBIGWEB::gotHit (attacker id, damage value, hit type, flags)?
Are there external engine-level filters (outside this script) that limit which hits invoke this process (e.g., only certain attack types), or is every hit forwarded to U8P_EWBIGWEB::gotHit?
Is Item::destroy(this) sufficient for all expected cleanup (visuals, loot drop, triggers), or are other systems expected to listen for the destroy event to perform additional behavior?

main_function.name: U8P_EWBIGWEB::gotHit
main_function.purpose: When the item/entity is hit, immediately remove/destroy the instance to perform cleanup and remove it from the game world.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine as the 'U8P_EWBIGWEB::gotHit' process for this object (processType 0x020C) when the object receives a hit or damage event.
No additional script-level conditions; the process performs its action unconditionally.

external_calls.purpose: Remove and clean up this item/entity instance from the game world (destroy 'this').

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_EWBIGWEB::gotHit is a process invoked when the entity/item is hit; its observable effect is to immediately destroy the instance.
